info.title: Nuvo Webhooks v0.0.5
info.description:
# Overview

Nuvo emits HTTP POST webhook events to customer-configured endpoints when specific events occur in the credit application system. This enables real-time event-driven integrations without polling.

## Webhook Event Types

Nuvo supports the following webhook event types:

1. **`CREDIT_APP_NEW`** - A new credit application has been submitted
2. **`CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE`** - Credit limit has been modified for an existing customer
3. **`CREDIT_APP_STATUS_CHANGE`** - Application status has changed (approval, rejection, etc.)
4. **`CUSTOMER_APP_SIGNATURE_REQUESTED`** - A signature has been requested on a customer application
5. **`MANUAL`** - Manually triggered webhook (used for testing and special cases)

## Request Format

- **Method**: POST
- **Content-Type**: application/json
- **Timeout**: 10 seconds (your endpoint must respond within this time)
- **Current version**: `0.0.9` (check `webhookVersion` in the payload)

**reviewStatus values**: `INCOMPLETE_APPLICATION`, `ACTIVE_ACCOUNT`, `PLACEHOLDER_APPLICATION`, `INCOMPLETE_ACCOUNT`, `APPLICATION`, `PLACEHOLDER_ACCOUNT`, `REMOVED_ACCOUNT`, `REVIEW_ACCOUNT`, `REJECTED_APPLICATION`

**Business type values**: `SOLE_PROPRIETORSHIP`, `PARTNERSHIP`, `LLC`, `COOPERATIVE`, `C_CORP`, `S_CORP`, `NON_PROFIT_ORGANIZATION`, `PUBLIC_AGENCY`

## Authentication & Security

Every webhook includes cryptographic signatures to verify authenticity:

**Headers:**

- `X-NUVO-CERT-URL`: https://www.nuvo.com/assets/webhook_public_key.pub
- `X-NUVO-SIGNATURE`: Base64-encoded ECDSA signature

**Algorithm Details:**

- Signature: ECDSA with curve secp384r1 (NIST P-384)
- Hash: SHA-256 of the JSON payload
- Encoding: ASN.1 DER format, Base64 encoded

A Node.js verification example is included as a code sample on every event below.

## Error Handling & Retry Logic

- **Maximum attempts**: 10 retries over ~13 hours
- **Backoff strategy**: Exponential backoff (90 seconds base, doubles each retry)
- **Retry triggers**: Any non-200 HTTP status, network failures, timeouts
- **Idempotency**: Use `eventUuid` to detect and skip duplicate events

After maximum retries, the webhook is marked as failed but your webhook remains active for future events.

## Monitoring & Troubleshooting

Once webhooks are enabled, you'll have access to a **Webhook Logs** tab in your Nuvo customer profile showing:

- **All webhook deliveries** with timestamps and status
- **Complete payloads** sent to your endpoint
- **Your endpoint responses** (status codes, headers, response body)
- **Retry attempts** and failure details

### Common Issues

1. **Webhooks not arriving**: Check your endpoint URL and firewall settings
2. **Timeouts**: Ensure your endpoint responds within 10 seconds
3. **Signature failures**: Verify you're using the correct public key and algorithm
4. **Duplicate processing**: Use `eventUuid` for deduplication

## Development & Testing

- **Use Nuvo's testing portal** (under Settings → Integrations)
- **Custom fields**: Two sections contain configurable fields that affect payload structure:
    - `additionalFieldResponses`: Custom credit application questions and responses
    - `internalFieldsValues`: Internal fields configured between supplier and Nuvo

post.description:
Sent when a new credit application is submitted by a buyer.

The payload contains the full buyer + creditApplication snapshot at
submission time. Use `eventUuid` to deduplicate retries.

post.description:
Sent when a credit limit is modified for an existing customer.

`creditApplication.defaultCreditLimit` reflects the new limit.

x-codeSamples.label: Example payload

post.description:
Sent when an application's review status changes (approval, rejection, etc.).

The new status is in `creditApplication.reviewStatus`.

ReviewStatus.title: ReviewStatus
ReviewStatus.description:
A Review Status tracks the state of information submitted from one company to another.
The most prominent example of this is Customer Applications, where buyers submit information to suppliers. Suppliers can use this information to onboard and manage the buyer (their customer).

Review Statuses follow these principles:
- Bidirectional visibility and transparency: both sides see the same status
- Certain actions can change the status, and there is a well defined state machine that governs these transitions.
- See `knowledge/review_status_state_machine.md` for more details.

uspsValidated.title: Uspsvalidated
uspsValidated.description: Whether the address has been USPS-validated.

addressType.description: Residential vs. business classification of the address.

recordType.description: USPS record type code from Smarty metadata.

businessType.title: Businesstype
businessType.description: One of SOLE_PROPRIETORSHIP, PARTNERSHIP, LLC, COOPERATIVE, C_CORP, S_CORP, NON_PROFIT_ORGANIZATION, PUBLIC_AGENCY.

internalId.title: Internalid
internalId.description: Customer-supplied internal id assigned to this buyer.

isVerified.title: Isverified
isVerified.description: Whether this buyer has been identity-verified.

nextReviewDate.title: Nextreviewdate
nextReviewDate.description: ISO 8601 date for the next scheduled review.

flow.title: Flow
flow.description: Configuration slug used for this application.

additionalFieldResponses.title: Additionalfieldresponses
additionalFieldResponses.description: Responses to custom credit application questions.

internalFieldsValues.title: Internalfieldsvalues
internalFieldsValues.description: Internal field values, organized as {section_key: {field_key: value}}.

referenceId.title: Referenceid
referenceId.description: Reference id supplied by the customer when the application was created.

isVerified.title: Isverified
isVerified.description: Whether this person has verified their identity.

registeredNames.title: Registerednames
registeredNames.description: Business names registered with the Secretary of State.

customSignatureKey.title: Customsignaturekey
customSignatureKey.description: Identifier for custom signature blocks (only present when kind='CUSTOM').

WebhookSmartyAddressRecordType.title: WebhookSmartyAddressRecordType
WebhookSmartyAddressRecordType.description:
USPS/Smarty record type code describing the matched address record.

Values mirror Smarty US Street `metadata.record_type`:
- F: Firm — Address for a specific company or organization
- G: General Delivery — Post office holds mail for pickup
- H: High-rise — Building with multiple units served by one delivery point
- P: PO Box — Post Office Box address
- R: Rural Route — Rural highway contract route
- S: Street — Standard street address
